Amit2009_DC_LPS.key_result: Half-life independence rho=0.154, bootstrap CI barely excludes zero

GSE221103_MYC.key_result: Half-life independence rho=0.203 raw, drops to 0.045 after controlling for expression

GSE54650_Liver.context: Baboon atlas study, mouse circadian tissue panel (Zhang et al. 2014)
GSE54650_Liver.key_result: Reference tissue for cross-tissue comparison, highest gating density

GSE54650_Heart.key_result: TEAD1 and YAP1 gated by all 13 clock genes, Hippo/YAP pathway dominance

GSE54650_Lung.key_result: Highest BMAL1-coupling density (14 genes), metabolic + cell cycle pathways

GSE54650_Kidney.key_result: Lowest gating density among 12 tissues, proliferation-only pathway

GSE54650_Muscle.key_result: Proliferation + cell cycle pathways, Myc and Wee1 key targets

GSE54650_Cerebellum.key_result: CDK1 exclusively gated by all 13 clock genes, medulloblastoma vulnerability

GSE54650_Hypothalamus.key_result: Metabolism + checkpoint pathways, Sirt1 and Chek2 targets

GSE54650_Brown_Fat.key_result: Cell cycle + DNA repair coupling, 6 BMAL1-coupled genes

GSE54650_Brainstem.key_result: Cell cycle + metabolic coupling, Ccnd1 top coupled gene

GSE54650_Adrenal.key_result: Cell cycle + metabolic coupling, 4 BMAL1-coupled genes

GSE54650_Aorta.key_result: Cell cycle pathway, Wee1 top coupled gene

GSE157357_WT.context: Intestinal organoid circadian model, wild type baseline
GSE157357_WT.key_result: Healthy gearbox with clock-target gap of 0.392

GSE157357_ApcKO.key_result: Hierarchy inverted, gap = -0.122, compensatory gating doubled to 22.4%

GSE157357_BmalKO.key_result: Hierarchy collapsed, gap = -0.082, LGR5 stem cell gating detected

GSE157357_DoubleKO.context: Double mutant, complete clock-cancer two-hit model

GSE39445_SufficientSleep.key_result: Baseline eigenvalue distribution for sleep comparison

GSE39445_SleepRestriction.key_result: Sleep restriction alters eigenvalue distribution, homeostatic pressure signature

GSE122541_DayShift.context: Shift work study, normal circadian alignment
GSE122541_DayShift.key_result: Day shift baseline for shift work comparison

GSE122541_NightShift.context: Chronic circadian misalignment via shift work
GSE122541_NightShift.key_result: Night shift alters eigenvalue distribution, chronic misalignment signature

GSE48113_Blood.context: Forced desynchrony protocol, human circadian transcriptomics
GSE48113_Blood.key_result: Human circadian hierarchy confirmed at blood transcriptome level

GSE98965_Baboon.context: Non-human primate circadian atlas (Mure et al. 2018)
GSE98965_Baboon.key_result: 70.5% of genes show higher persistence in at least one tissue, cross-species hierarchy confirmed

GSE201207_YoungKidney.key_result: Young kidney circadian architecture, distinct from liver eigenvalue profile

GSE242964_Arabidopsis.context: Plant circadian transcriptomics, 3 biological replicates per timepoint
GSE242964_Arabidopsis.key_result: Plant circadian hierarchy confirms cross-kingdom conservation of AR(2) eigenvalue organization

GSE3431_YeastMetabolicCycle.key_result: Metabolic cycle AR(2) eigenvalues show oscillatory persistence hierarchy in single-celled organism

Tu2005_YeastMetabolicCycle.context: Yeast metabolic oscillation, curated gene set (Tu et al. 2005)
Tu2005_YeastMetabolicCycle.key_result: Half-life independence confirmed in yeast: rho=0.018 (n=4887 matched genes)

Arbeitman2002_Drosophila.context: Drosophila embryonic development time-series (Arbeitman et al. 2002)
Arbeitman2002_Drosophila.key_result: Half-life independence confirmed: rho=-0.003 (n=3241 matched genes), developmental persistence hierarchy

Zaas2009_Influenza.context: Human influenza infection time-course (Zaas et al. 2009)
Zaas2009_Influenza.key_result: Half-life independence confirmed: rho=0.009 (n=8456 matched genes), immune response AR(2) hierarchy

Robles2014_LiverProteome.key_result: Protein-level eigenvalues confirm mRNA-level hierarchy, post-transcriptional reinforcement of clock persistence

HumanPlasmaProteome.key_result: Protein-level circadian hierarchy in human plasma

OtobeYoshitane2026_LiverProteome.key_result: Independent proteomics replication of liver eigenvalue hierarchy

resolution_robustness.claim: AR(2) eigenvalue estimates are robust to 2× resolution change (1h vs 2h)
resolution_robustness.evidence: Clock mean |λ| identical (0.7655 vs 0.7654), genome-wide differs by only 0.029
resolution_robustness.caveat: AR(2) preference drops from 95.6% to 52.9% with fewer timepoints (statistical power issue, not eigenvalue bias)

translational_amplification.claim: Translational regulation may amplify circadian persistence hierarchy
translational_amplification.evidence: Ribosome footprint clock mean |λ|=0.8506 vs total RNA 0.7654, gap 0.2455 vs 0.2069
translational_amplification.caveat: Single dataset, no statistical test of RNA vs ribosome difference, could reflect translational buffering

human_disruption_direction.caveat: Only 6 sleep dep timepoints (marginal for AR(2)), opposite to naive disruption prediction, may reflect homeostatic pressure buildup

hierarchy_universality.claim: Clock > target hierarchy is preserved across datasets and resolutions
hierarchy_universality.evidence: All 3 mouse liver analyses show clock > target: gaps +0.13, +0.21, +0.25
hierarchy_universality.caveat: Only tested in mouse liver; no gene-level hierarchy test in human blood (probe IDs)

literature_validation.claim: 59 literature-curated circadian genes are recovered at 98.3% rate across 21 datasets
literature_validation.evidence: 58/59 genes recovered, only Tp53 missed (post-translational regulation), sources include Panda 2002, Matsuo 2003, Kang 2009
literature_validation.caveat: Tp53 regulation is primarily post-translational, invisible to mRNA-level AR(2)

falsification_test.claim: AR(2) eigenvalue hierarchy is not an artifact of gene selection
falsification_test.evidence: Arntl coupling rate 8.4% vs Gapdh 0.3% vs random 0.0-0.5%, enrichment ratio ~180x
falsification_test.caveat: Falsification is necessary but not sufficient for causal claims

bias_audit.claim: AR(2) rankings are free from systematic biases
bias_audit.evidence: Three automated tests all pass: time-shuffle destruction (p<0.001), irrelevant metric correlation (|r|<0.05), expression-matched null hierarchy (gap persists)
bias_audit.caveat: Tests cover known bias classes; unknown confounds remain possible

non_circadian_hierarchy.claim: Regulator-to-effector eigenvalue hierarchy generalizes beyond circadian biology
non_circadian_hierarchy.evidence: Rabani 2014 DC LPS response: fast responders (Il1b |λ|=0.55, Junb |λ|=0.43) < sustained effectors (Stat1 |λ|=0.99, Ifit1 |λ|=0.80)
non_circadian_hierarchy.caveat: Single non-circadian dataset; needs replication in other temporal systems

halflife_independence_replication.claim: mRNA half-life and AR(2) eigenvalue are independent properties across 7 datasets (22,989 genes)
halflife_independence_replication.evidence: Weighted mean Spearman rho=0.0115 across 7 datasets. Large circadian datasets (GSE11923 n=5945, Tu2005 n=4887, Arbeitman2002 n=3241, Zaas2009 n=8456) show rho near zero. Short time-series datasets (Rabani rho=0.13, Amit rho=0.15, GSE221103 rho=0.20) show weak correlations that dissolve under controls.
halflife_independence_replication.caveat: Weak correlations in short time-series (7-14 timepoints) are artifacts of explosive eigenvalue inclusion and expression-level confounding. Independence claim requires >=24 timepoints and n>1000.

before_after_trajectories.claim: Eigenvalue trajectories shift systematically under biological perturbations
before_after_trajectories.evidence: 6 pre-loaded comparison pairs covering immune activation, oncogene toggle, cancer initiation, sleep restriction, shift work, and clock knockout
before_after_trajectories.caveat: Shift magnitudes depend on dataset quality and timepoint count

cross_species_conservation.claim: AR(2) eigenvalue distributions are conserved across kingdoms of life
cross_species_conservation.evidence: Mean |λ| ranges from 0.48 (yeast) to 0.55 (Drosophila) across 6 species: mouse (12 tissues), baboon (multi-tissue atlas), yeast (metabolic cycle), Arabidopsis (seedling), Drosophila (embryo), human (blood, cancer, proteome)
cross_species_conservation.caveat: Cross-kingdom comparison uses heterogeneous experimental designs and temporal resolutions

proteome_concordance.claim: Clock gene eigenvalue hierarchy is preserved at the protein level
proteome_concordance.evidence: Three independent proteomics datasets (Robles 2014 n=3071, human plasma n=137, Otobe-Yoshitane 2026 n=151) show consistent protein-level eigenvalue rankings matching mRNA predictions
proteome_concordance.caveat: Proteomics datasets have fewer timepoints and genes than transcriptomic counterparts

twelve_tissue_atlas.claim: Tissue-specific circadian defense signatures are quantitatively distinct
twelve_tissue_atlas.evidence: GSE54650 12-tissue panel shows gating rates from 2% (kidney) to 16.4% (liver), with tissue-specific pathway dominance (liver: DNA repair, heart: Hippo/YAP, cerebellum: mitosis-only)
twelve_tissue_atlas.caveat: Single study design (Zhang et al. 2014); independent replication across tissues needed

organoid_perturbation_model.claim: APC-KO inverts and BMAL1-KO collapses the circadian eigenvalue hierarchy
organoid_perturbation_model.evidence: GSE157357 four-genotype panel: WT gap=+0.392, APC-KO gap=-0.122 (inverted), BMAL1-KO gap=-0.082 (collapsed), double-KO shows 17-fold gating collapse
organoid_perturbation_model.caveat: In vitro organoid system; in vivo confirmation needed

plant_circadian_hierarchy.claim: Arabidopsis thaliana shows AR(2) eigenvalue hierarchy consistent with animal circadian systems
plant_circadian_hierarchy.evidence: GSE242964 three-day panel (40,812 genes per day) shows conserved eigenvalue distribution in plant circadian transcriptomics
plant_circadian_hierarchy.caveat: Plant clock architecture differs fundamentally from animal TTFL; convergence may reflect universal oscillator properties rather than homology

human_blood_circadian.claim: Human blood transcriptomics supports circadian eigenvalue hierarchy
human_blood_circadian.evidence: Four human blood datasets (GSE48113, GSE113883, GSE39445, GSE122541) consistently show eigenvalue hierarchy in human peripheral blood
human_blood_circadian.caveat: Blood is a mixed-cell-type tissue; eigenvalue estimates reflect composite signals

state_space_comparison.claim: AR(2) OLS eigenvalue rankings are concordant with state-space model alternatives
state_space_comparison.evidence: SARIMAX AR(2) MLE produces mean rank correlation rho=0.997 across 3 datasets (strong estimator robustness). Local Level structural time-series produces mean rho=0.423 (moderate model-class robustness). Hierarchy preserved under AR(2) regardless of estimator; partially preserved under Local Level (4/5). Derived |lambda| more stable than raw coefficients.
state_space_comparison.caveat: Local Level model captures a different construct (noise-separated persistence); moderate concordance expected and interpretable, not concerning. SARIMAX convergence rate 53-66% censors near-critical genes at unit root boundary. AIC comparison between OLS and state-space is not strictly apples-to-apples (different likelihood formulations).

decomposition_stability.claim: The AR(2) eigenvalue hierarchy is robust to the choice of decomposition method for tissues with moderate initial gaps
decomposition_stability.evidence: 6 methods (raw, mean, median, PC1, var25, var50) tested across 11 datasets. Global DSI=0.527, mean rank correlation=0.619. Sign preserved in 8/11 datasets. Gold-standard GSE11923 DSI=0.897.
decomposition_stability.caveat: WEAK by strict stability criteria; failed in Cerebellum (DSI=0.08), Aorta (DSI=0.50), and APC-KO (DSI=-0.44).

affected_convergence_points.update: Test E confirmed 25-30% nonlinear deviation is universal across all ODE models
affected_convergence_points.new_confidence: Claims about exact |λ| values should include ±25-30% uncertainty from linearization
AR(2) cannot detect primary Boman disease mechanism
affected_convergence_points.still_valid: Direction of APC-KO perturbation correctly predicted

affected_convergence_points.update: 21-34% deviation found in ALL five canonical ODE models
affected_convergence_points.new_confidence: This is a universal property of linearized observation, not a Boman-specific failure
affected_convergence_points.still_valid: AR(2) captures dynamical class even with quantitative bias

test_e_updates.unaffected_convergence_points: Most of 22 convergence mappings remain valid because they rely on relative comparisons (clock>target ordering, cross-species hierarchy, disruption direction) which survive nonlinear distortion

fibonacci_reply_validation.description: Systematic cross-validation of Reply paper claims against all platform datasets
fibonacci_reply_validation.key_amendment: Nguyen, Lausten and Boman 2025 (Cells 14:1428) integrated — co-authored by Boman himself, provides tuft cell CRC data, NOTCH/WNT pathway details, and M-cell terminology clarification

organoid_evidence.interpretation: APC-KO inverts hierarchy (WNT constitutive), BMAL1-KO collapses it (phase source lost)
